titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the 315 Ditmas Avenue photograph 2025.037
titlestmt.author: Dee Bowers

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-05-26 14:02:17 +0000.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

did.abstract: One 5x7 modern reproduction of a circa 1915-1925 sepia-toned photographic print of a house at 315 Ditmas Avenue.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Tiger Gardiner and Richard Casbianca, 2025.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection consists of a single item.

bioghist.head: Historical note
bioghist.p: The Brooklyn neighborhood of Kensington was once part of the Dutch town of Flatbush. The first European colonizers in the area settled in 1737. It remained rural until the Coney Island Plank Road opened in 1850. After Ocean Parkway, which bisects the current neighborhood, was completed in 1876, development of the area accelerated rapidly. Much of its current housing stock, potentially including the home pictured in this photograph, was built in the 1920s. From the 1980s onwards the neighborhood has attracted a diverse array of immigrants comprising various nationalities, ethnicities, and religious affiliations.

list.head: Source:
list.item: Gallgher, John J. and John Manbeck. "Kensington." In The Encyclopedia of New York City, edited by Kenneth T. Jackson, 696. New Haven, Conn.: Yale University Press; New York: New-York Historical Society, 2010.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: If digital surrogates exist, they should be used in place of the originals whenever possible.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: 315 Ditmas Avenue photograph, circa 1915-1925, 2025.037; Brooklyn Public Library, Center for Brooklyn History.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Kensington photographs in the Brooklyn location scouting photographs collection (CBHM.0017) and the Brooklyn Daily Eagle photographs (BCMS.0002)
Kensington folders in the Brooklyn, Queens, and Long Island real estate brochure collection (ARC.296) and the Brooklyn Historical Society ephemera collection (ARC.272)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One 5x7 modern reproduction of a circa 1915-1925 sepia-toned photographic print of a house at 315 Ditmas Avenue. Two smalls boys on the porch are identified as Norman and Robert. The house is located between East 3rd and 4th Streets in the Kensington neighborhood. As of 2026, the house is still standing, though it has been altered somewhat.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: This item is in the public domain. While many items at the Center for Brooklyn History are unrestricted, we do not own reproduction rights to all materials. Be aware of the several kinds of rights that might apply: copyright, licensing and trademarks. The researcher assumes all responsibility for copyright questions.
